free_text: Cross sections for the spallation production of Be-10
in targets of N, Mg, Si and their astrophysical
The Mg target was made up of 10 pieces of 99.9% pure
metal foil, each 0.5 mm thick and 4 cm**2 in area.
The Si was in the form of approx. 500 mg of 99.99% pure
powder wrapped in thin gold foil. Each of the nitrogen
targets consisted of  approx. 5 g of high purity
vanadium nitride powder that had been compressed into
disks 20 mm in diameter and 0.5 mm in thickness.
For Be-7 production-target stack consisting of 25-mm
diameter disks of Al 3*21.2 mg/cm**2, Si 54.1 mg/cm**2,
Al 21.2 mg/cm**2, Mg 79.4 mg/cm**2 and Al 21.2 mg/cm**2
Corrections due to the contribution of the V in V(N)
* the uncertainty in the counter efficiencies
* the uncertainties in the relative separator
SD: Institute code in FACILITY corrected.
Authors have estimated 7Be cross sections in V on the
basis of measurements on the neighboring target Ti
